constraints: Reveal rooms and connections through exploration
Do not reveal undiscovered secrets
Keep guidance light and avoid GPS-style navigation
Target skill-based top-down combat players
Support casual through mastery players with meaningful difficulty tailoring
Do not rely only on health and damage multipliers
Complete MVP campaign saves progression and difficulty work offline
Multiplayer follows only after a successful MVP
Preserve clean extension boundaries without MVP networking complexity
Co-op campaign combat exploration objectives and bosses are the first multiplayer direction
Leaderboards remain post-MVP
No networking scaling shared progression or leaderboard service in MVP
Windows PC polished MVP first
Keyboard mouse and gamepad support
Android follows after PC stability
Keep actions platform-neutral UI scalable and saves mobile-compatible
Exploratory first clear roughly 45 to 75 minutes
Mastered run roughly 20 to 35 minutes
Natural checkpoint sections roughly 10 to 20 minutes
Prioritize distinct replayable content over filler
Core layouts bosses major encounters and competitive categories remain deterministic
Allow bounded authored modifiers and variants
Preserve mastery and fair speedrunning
Do not convert campaign into a heavily randomized roguelike
Weapons mainly come from floor pickups and tiered random strongboxes
Shops provide a randomized purchasable inventory
Random discovery is a central source of excitement
Random results are bounded by progression
Duplicates remain possible and useful
Shops may expose roughly 40 progression-appropriate slots
Weapon types enter the pool gradually
Boxes use nearby progression windows
Stars augments and enchantments create variants
Use soft protection against pathological bad luck
Weapon families return as authored stronger successors
Successors may add modest mechanical changes
Complex and inherently stronger archetypes unlock later
Deliberate power inflation is planned and paced
Late archetypes must add novelty complexity or power
Shop stock remains stable until deliberately refreshed
Rerolls are limited and increasingly expensive
A small number of items may be locked through rerolls
Prevent cheap infinite perfect-roll fishing
Current direction uses eight strongbox tiers and two-star ten-level augments
Elite high-augment gear primarily comes from high-tier floor boxes
Exact weapon and enchantments remain random
Extreme shop rolls occur only at screenshot-worthy rarity
High box tiers unlock progressively
Exact odds require playtesting
Player level and campaign progress hard-cap eligible box tiers
Difficulty objectives bosses and mastery improve high-tier odds
Every level retains a non-zero chance for unlocked tiers
Earlier levels have lower odds and weaker quality distributions
Later difficult content provides materially better expected rewards
Each box tier has a quality floor and weighted expected band
Neighboring tiers overlap with limited jackpot tails
Older lower-base-level weapons may roll excellent stars and augments
Balance base level family power modifiers and synergy together
Low-tier farming must not overpower current progression
Exceptional modifiers may bridge several progression levels
Strong successors eventually overtake older jackpots
New weapons remain meaningful
Older weapons retain situational value through handling effects matchups or synergy
No permanent universal best-in-slot outliers
Comparison UI communicates contextual strengths
Ordinary shop weapons are attainable but consequential purchases
Stars augments enchantments and later families sharply raise prices
Jackpots may require several runs of savings
Selling and dismantling return partial value
Purchases rerolls and services compete for core currency
Exact prices and income require playtesting
One consumed token reserves one exact shop item
The reservation survives rerolls visits and completed runs
Only one profile-wide reservation exists
Reserved item keeps its original price
No expiry until purchase or deliberate discard
Ordinary reroll locks remain separate and short-term
Unwanted weapons may be sold for currency or dismantled for bounded materials
Material yields reflect level rarity stars augments and enchantment categories
Materials support controlled improvement rerolling utility crafting or later systems
Do not allow free perfect-item manufacturing
Both paths return partial value and resist farming exploits
Provide favourites locks bulk marking auto-junk sorting comparison and keep-best-copy tools
Do not make direct duplicate fusion the primary upgrade path

recovered_state.rule: Re-verify reconstructed details before final requirements

open_questions: Persistent armory and carried-in-level storage model
Stack and technical architecture after product scope is mature

readiness.reason: High-weight Product Discovery decisions and recovery verification remain incomplete

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether weapon storage is tightly limited everywhere, uses a generous persistent armory with a smaller carried field inventory and checkpoint transfer rules, or allows unlimited universal storage and swapping anywhere. Include loot-flow, combat interruption, collection, and mobile-interface implications. Put the agent recommendation after all options at the end of the card. Persist the accepted answer and update the handoff before continuing.
